summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several specific developments in small UAS activity. Ukraine has unveiled the Gnome-NS, a new ground-based "mother drone" designed to carry and deploy FPV drones, and also introduced the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone. Russian forces have deployed fiber-optic FPV drones in Kharkiv for the first time since the full-scale invasion, with one hitting the city and another striking Perelyub village near the Chernihiv border. Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to knock out a Russian Kasta-2E2 radar in Zaporizhzhia, while a Russian FPV drone strike in Orikhiv injured a 67-year-old civilian. Ukraine’s $880 million drone production line has significantly degraded Russian frontline movement, and Motor-G is now shipping 200,000 drone motors per month, making it Ukraine’s largest engine manufacturer. Additionally, Ukraine’s I-SEE system can track Russian drones in real time without emitting radio signals, and the GUR has published detailed teardowns of Russian drones and missiles, naming every foreign component inside.
